question: How many use cases have we delivered for Banking clients?
ground_truth_answer: We have delivered 7 use cases for Banking clients. These span across AI & Machine Learning (Credit Risk Scoring, Fraud Detection, and Customer Churn Prediction), Data Engineering (Enterprise Data Lakehouse Migration, Basel III Regulatory Reporting Pipeline, and Unified Customer Data Platform), and Digital Transformation (End-to-End Digital Transformation of Retail Banking Operations).

question: Provide me the 3 AI ML use cases we have delivered.
ground_truth_answer: The top 3 AI & ML use cases to showcase are: 1) AI-Powered Credit Risk Scoring for BankNova - improved the Gini coefficient from 0.42 to 0.67, reduced false rejection rates from 34% to 11%, and unlocked $23M in incremental annual loan revenue within 8 months. 2) Real-Time Transaction Fraud Detection for NexBank - reduced annual fraud losses from $41M to $14M (66% reduction) and detection latency from 4-6 hours to under 200ms, processing 12 million+ transactions daily. 3) Intelligent Claims Automation & Leakage Detection for ShieldGen Insurance - reduced claims cycle time from 18 to 7.2 days, achieved 52% auto-adjudication, and saved $14M in claims leakage in Year 1. All three demonstrate rapid delivery (7-9 months) with measurable, quantified business impact.

question: I am meeting a CFO of a bank next week who is worried about regulatory penalties and compliance reporting delays. Do we have a relevant reference use case?
ground_truth_answer: Absolutely. Our most relevant reference is the Real-Time Regulatory Reporting Pipeline for Basel III Compliance delivered for EuroCapital Bank - a mid-size private bank with cross-border operations across 6 European countries. The client had missed 3 regulatory submission deadlines in 18 months, incurring EUR2.1M in penalties and heightened ECB oversight. Nexvance built a fully automated BCBS 239-compliant reporting pipeline that reduced submission time from 8 days to 18 hours post period-end, eliminated all missed deadlines for 14 consecutive months after go-live, and cut manual analyst effort by 74%. Data quality error rates dropped from 6.8% to 0.3%. This is a strong CFO narrative - it removed regulatory risk, freed analyst capacity, and saved EUR2.1M in penalties.

question: I need to pitch our fraud detection capabilities to a prospect. What is the strongest outcome we can talk about?
ground_truth_answer: Our strongest fraud outcome is from the Real-Time Transaction Fraud Detection engagement for NexBank - one of South Asia's top-5 national banks by asset size with ~28,000 employees. The headline numbers are compelling: we reduced annual fraud losses from $41M to $14M - a $27M saving in the first year alone, representing a 66% reduction. At the same time, we cut false positives from 78,000 to ~22,500 per month (71% reduction), which significantly improved customer experience by eliminating erroneous transaction blocks. Detection latency went from 4-6 hours to real-time under 200ms, processing 12 million+ transactions daily. The fraud ops team's case resolution time also improved from 6.2 days to 1.8 days. This is a strong story combining revenue protection, customer satisfaction, and operational efficiency.

question: I am going into a meeting with a retail bank's Chief Digital Officer. Which use case should I lead with?
ground_truth_answer: Lead with the FutureBank Digital Transformation engagement - it is our most comprehensive and impactful case for a Chief Digital Officer audience. FutureBank was an established private sector bank with 180+ branches that had only 18% digital channel adoption and was losing market share at 3.2% per year to digital-first challenger banks. This is an award-winning case recognized at the Asia Banking Awards 2024.

question: A prospect CISO is asking whether we have experience improving security posture for a large financial firm. What can we show them?
ground_truth_answer: We have a directly relevant reference for a CISO. We delivered a Zero-Trust Security Architecture Implementation for ApexCapital Advisors - a large asset management firm managing $180 billion in AUM with ~4,500 employees in North America. The firm had suffered two significant security incidents including a lateral movement attack where an attacker traversed the internal network undetected for 22 days. Nexvance implemented a full five-pillar Zero Trust architecture (Identity, Device, Network, Application, Threat Detection) aligned to NIST SP 800-207. The results: MTTD dropped from 22 days to 4.2 hours, MTTR from 9 days to 6.3 hours, attack surface reduced by 68% by eliminating VPN, and SOC 2 Type II was passed with zero exceptions for the first time in the firm's history. Cyber insurance premiums were reduced by 28% ($1.4M annually), and the firm onboarded 3 new institutional clients worth $340M in AUM who mandated Zero Trust as a due diligence requirement.

question: Do we have experience helping banks improve their customer retention and reduce churn?
ground_truth_answer: Yes, we have a dedicated use case for customer retention. We built an AI-Driven Customer Churn Prediction & Retention Engine for CrescentBank - a mid-size private sector bank with ~6,800 employees in the Middle East & North Africa. The bank was losing 19% of retail customers annually (well above the 11% regional average) at a cost of $28M per year. In 7 months, Nexvance deployed a predictive model that identified at-risk customers 90 days in advance and triggered automated interventions (personalized offers, relationship manager outreach, digital nudges) through Salesforce. The churn rate dropped from 19% to 12.4%, campaign ROI improved from 1.4x to 4.7x, and $16.5M in revenue was protected in Year 1 - all while reducing marketing spend by 37%.
